description:
You are a technical writer creating documentation for a REST API used by a library management system. Using TiddlyWiki at http://localhost:8080, build a complete API documentation wiki by creating five specific tiddlers. Each tiddler must be tagged with 'API-Documentation' and 'LibrarySystem', contain at least 120 words, and include at least one table and internal wikilinks to other tiddlers in the set. 

The 'Library API - Overview' tiddler should introduce the API, covering its purpose, base URL structure, versioning, data formats, rate limiting, and pagination, including a resource summary table with columns for Resource, Description, Base Endpoint, and Supported Methods. The 'Library API - Authentication Guide' must document API key management, Bearer tokens, and OAuth 2.0 scopes, featuring an authentication methods table (Method, When to Use, How to Include in Request, Expiry/Rotation Policy) and a code example for an HTTP request header using monospace formatting. The 'Library API - Endpoints Reference' needs to document at least 8 REST endpoints across books, patrons, loans, and reservations, using a detailed endpoint table (HTTP Method, Endpoint Path, Description, Auth Required, Request Body Fields, Response Fields) and grouping by resource type with headings. The 'Library API - Error Codes Reference' must include a table (HTTP Status, Error Code, Error Message, Description, How to Resolve) covering status codes 400, 401, 403, 404, 409, 422, 429, and 500. Finally, the 'Library API - Changelog' should document at least 4 versions in a table (Version, Release Date, Type, Changes Summary, Breaking Changes), using headings to separate major versions. No login is required.
